pr.title: lb: match fn_80026650 (100%), improve fn_800268B4 and lb_8000FD48
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Matched `fn_80026650` in `lbaudio_ax.c` by using direct named extern arrays instead of fields off the `lbl_80433710` struct anchor, modestly improved `fn_800268B4` with the same extern swap plus a pointer-increment loop form, and improved `lb_8000FD48` by extracting repeated `DynamicsData` free-list pop logic into `popDynamicsData`. The PR was verified with `python configure.py && ninja` and per-function `tools/checkdiff.py`; the slice contains no human review comments.

key_files.role: Matched `fn_80026650` and improved `fn_800268B4` by replacing `lbl_80433710.x194` / `lbl_80433710.x274` accesses with direct externs `lbl_804338A4` / `lbl_80433984`, and by changing `fn_800268B4` to pointer-increment array traversal.
Improved `lb_8000FD48` by replacing duplicated free-list pop code with a `static inline struct DynamicsData* popDynamicsData(void)` helper.

what_changed: `fn_80026650` changed local array pointers from `lbl_80433710.x194` and `lbl_80433710.x274` to direct named externs `lbl_804338A4` and `lbl_80433984`; PR body reports this moved the function to 100%.
`fn_800268B4` now initializes `arr_38a4` and `arr_3984` from `lbl_804338A4` and `lbl_80433984` instead of the `lbl_80433710` struct anchor.

`fn_800268B4` loop changed from indexed accesses like `arr_38a4[i]`, `arr_3984[i]`, and `arr_4e4[i][0]` to dereference-and-increment style using `*arr_38a4`, `*arr_3984`, `(*arr_4e4)[0]`, followed by `arr_38a4++`, `arr_3984++`, and `arr_4e4++`.
`lb_8000FD48` removed two duplicated blocks that popped `cur_data` and nulled `entry->next`, replacing them with `popDynamicsData()` calls for the head and subsequent list nodes.
The decomp.dev bot reported one new match, `main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax` `fn_80026650`, and two unmatched-item improvements: `lb_8000FD48` and `fn_800268B4`.
smart_moves: Used direct named externs for the two target arrays when struct-field access through `lbl_80433710` generated worse code; this is explicitly credited in the PR body for the `fn_80026650` match.
For `fn_800268B4`, matched the apparent original loop shape by carrying moving pointers through the loop rather than repeatedly indexing from base pointers.
Extracted the free-list pop into a small `static inline` helper, preserving the null check, `cur_data` advance, and `entry->next = NULL` side effect while improving `lb_8000FD48` codegen.
Mirrored an existing inline-helper pattern (`inlineA0`, per PR body) instead of introducing an unrelated control-flow style.
Recorded both build verification and per-function `checkdiff.py` verification, making the match-percent claims auditable.
decomp_lessons: When a global struct anchor is only a convenience overlay, direct named externs for individual arrays can produce better address-generation/codegen and may unlock a match.
For fixed-count array scans, try pointer-increment loops (`*ptr` plus `ptr++`) and `i != 0` countdown conditions when the target assembly suggests a CTR loop.

Small `static inline` helpers can improve matching when repeated source blocks need to compile as the same inlined sequence; this worked for the repeated `DynamicsData` free-list pop in `lb_8000FD48`.
Do not treat a semantically clearer indexed loop as always equivalent for matching; the chosen source expression form can affect prologue and loop layout.
Keep function-specific match percentages in the PR body and confirm them with `tools/checkdiff.py`, especially when only small byte improvements are expected.
naming_conventions: The PR intentionally uses address-style named externs `lbl_804338A4` and `lbl_80433984` directly rather than accessing them as `lbl_80433710.x194` and `lbl_80433710.x274`.
`popDynamicsData` is a descriptive static inline helper name for removing one node from the `DynamicsData` free list headed by `cur_data`.
Existing anonymous/address labels such as `lbl_804D6450`, `lbl_804D644C`, `lbl_804D6448`, and `offsets_arr_803BC4E4` were left intact; the PR did not attempt broader semantic renaming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Loop-shape tactic in `fn_800268B4`: `for (i = 0x37; i != 0; i--)` with pointer increments, rather than `for (i = 0x37; i > 0; i--)` with indexed array loads.
PR body states the pointer-increment form made the `fn_800268B4` prologue use `bdnz` / `mtctr` CTR-loop layout and improved the function from 88.5% to 88.7%.
Inline-helper tactic in `lb_8000FD48`: move repeated free-list pop code into `static inline popDynamicsData()` to alter inlining/code shape while keeping behavior.
Verification tactic: run `python configure.py && ninja`, then recheck each touched function with `tools/checkdiff.py`.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews are present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code at 67.17% overall, +0.01% and +352 bytes for GALE01.
decomp.dev bot reported `fn_80026650` improved from 96.08% to 100.00% in `main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax`.
decomp.dev bot reported `lb_8000FD48` improved from 93.66% to 94.19% and `fn_800268B4` improved from 88.47% to 88.71%.
follow_up_queries: fn_800268B4 pointer increment bdnz mtctr arr_38a4 arr_3984 arr_4e4
